description: BASELINE: 6-layer ternary GPT (fast-A geometry: hidden=512, heads=8, ff=1408, ~44.7M params, 19.3M trits + 25.2M FP embeddings). Recipe transferred from HRM champion: random scales lognormal frozen, freeze non-embed FP, CMuon-STE cautious lr=0.20 cosine, default 70/25/5 mix, 5000 steps. val 4.0645 — BEATS HRM r042 Phase A (same recipe at fast-A) by -0.073 nats AT HALF THE WALL TIME (7877s vs ~15000s for HRM). Confirms recurrence-free GPT is the right autoresearch substrate for ternary speedups. Trajectory still descending at step 5000 (last 500 -0.027). New ternary-nanoGPT leaderboard floor.

Q-K=V (arxiv 2606.04032): share W_K and W_V projections. Param count 44.74M → 43.14M (-1.60M, -8pct of trits). val 4.1335 vs g001 4.0645 = +0.069 nats (+1.7pct PPL, well below the +5pct pass bar; paper reported +2.48pct at 1.2B). Wall 7691s vs g001 7877s = -2.4pct (one fewer projection per attention block). PROMOTED to baseline: all future runs use --share-kv unless explicitly ablated.

Phase 1 #2: CMuon momentum dtype bf16 (was fp32). val 4.1281 vs g002 4.1335 = -0.005 nats (noise). Wall +180s (~+2.3pct, slight dtype-cast overhead in EMA update). PROMOTED to baseline. Memory: CMuon m buffer for 17.7M trits drops 70.8MB→35.4MB. bf16 dtype keeps fp32 range; only mantissa truncated. NS5 still fp32 internally.

CMuon LR sweep leg 1: muon-lr=0.30 (up from g003's 0.20). val 4.1609 vs g003 4.1281 = +0.033 nats (+0.8pct). lr=0.30 WORSE → optimum at or below 0.20, sweep down to 0.15 next. Note: SIGINT'd at step 4430 to validate resume path; trainer auto-resumed cleanly from interrupted.pt and ran to step 5000. Discovered minor harness issue: tee truncated train.log on resume (lost pre-SIGINT vals); fixed with tee -a in this commit. Wall is approximate combined (7063s pre + 1141s post). Discard from leaderboard.

CMuon LR sweep leg 2: muon-lr=0.15 (down from g003's 0.20). val 4.1147 vs g003 4.1281 = -0.013 nats (best so far). 0.15 IS better than 0.20 → optimum below 0.20, continue down to 0.10. Hybrid run: bs=2 ga=16 for first 3635 steps (1.55s/it), bs=4 ga=8 for the resume tail (much faster wall, same effective batch 32). interrupted.pt-based resume across batch shape change works. tee -a harness fix preserved both segments in train.log. bs=4 ga=8 adopted as new baseline going forward.

CMuon LR sweep leg 3: muon-lr=0.10 with bs=4 ga=8 (full bs=4 from scratch). val 4.1327 vs g005 0.15 = +0.018 nats (worse). LR optimum BRACKETED: 0.30 (4.16) > 0.20 (4.13) > 0.15 (4.11) > 0.10 (4.13) — g005 lr=0.15 is the GPT optimum. Wall 7565s ≈ g005's combined ~7773s and g003's bs=2 7877s — bs=4 saves ~5pct kernel-launch overhead at this scale, not 2x. Total wall is dominated by tokens-per-step which is constant across bs/ga splits of effective batch 32. Discarded from leaderboard.

Phase 1 #7b: effective-batch sweep. lr=0.15 + bs=2 ga=32 (eff batch 64 vs g005's 32). val 4.0085 vs g005 4.1147 = -0.106 nats (-2.5pct) — NEW CHAMPION at fixed-step protocol. Wall 15172s = 2x g005 (linear in tokens per step at fixed step count). Better val per step, but per-WALL the tradeoff may not be favorable; eff=32 at 10000 steps would likely beat eff=64 at 5000 steps for similar wall. Keeping eff=32 as practical baseline (faster iteration), recording eff=64 as best-known reference.

Phase 5a: TRITISE THE EMBEDDINGS. 42.9M of 43.5M params now ternary (98.6pct ternary, FP residual 0.68M = norms + scales). val 4.7801 vs g005 baseline 4.1147 = +0.665 nats (+16pct). Trajectory still descending at step 5000 (-0.027 last 500). KEY: trajectory SLOPES are IDENTICAL (g008 -1.46 nats / 4500 steps vs g005 -1.46 / 4500). The gap is a FLAT OFFSET from worse init — random ternary embedding starts at val 6.24 vs random-normal embedding 5.57. Training dynamics are equivalent. Model trains cleanly, no collapse. Next: test whether unfrozen scales close the offset, then try longer training.

Phase 5a follow-up: drop --freeze-scales so embedding scales (and all QLinear scales) are Lion-trainable. val 4.3840 vs g008 4.7801 = -0.396 nats (closed 60pct of the gap to g005 baseline 4.1147). 42.9M trits + 0.67M trainable FP scales = 98.5pct ternary, FP residual matches the user's 'block scales where trits not sufficient' goal. Trajectory still descending at step 5000 with steep slope (-0.049/500 vs g005's -0.027/500), suggesting more compute will close the remaining 0.27 nat gap. Wall 8417s. NEW best at trit-emb leaderboard. Phase 5a recipe is now: --random-scales --trit-embeddings (no --freeze-scales).

Phase 5a final test: trit-embeddings + unfrozen scales at 10000 steps. val 4.1609 — within 0.046 nats of g005 FP-embed at 5000 steps. Trajectory still descending at end (-0.053 last 500). 98.5pct ternary model essentially matches the FP-embed model at 2x compute. Phase 5a is functionally complete. Residual gap of ~0.2-0.3 nats at matched compute is the precision tax at fast-A scale. The recipe is locked: --trit-embeddings + unfrozen scales (drop --freeze-scales).

Phase 5 follow-up: drop --random-scales (unit-scale init for trainable scales). val 4.3731 vs g009 4.3840 = -0.011 nats (noise). Trajectory marginally better at end, marginally worse at start. Conclusion: init scheme for scales is not the lever. Adopt unit-scale init as default for the trit-emb recipe (cleaner / one fewer flag).

Phase 5 LR sweep: trit-emb + lr=0.10 (down from g011's 0.15). val 4.4499 vs g011 4.3731 = +0.077 nats (worse). lr=0.15 stays optimum for trit-emb regime, same as FP-emb regime. LR optimum does not shift between regimes.

COMPOUND wins: trit-emb (Phase 5a) + eff batch 64 (Phase 1 #7b). val 4.2016 vs g011 trit-emb eff=32 4.3731 = -0.172 nats. The eff=64 benefit is LARGER for trit-emb (-0.172) than for FP-emb (-0.106 from g005→g007), so the wins are super-additive. New Phase 5 champion at fixed compute. Gap to FP baseline (g007 4.0085) shrinks to +0.193 nats. Wall 16797s (2x eff=32). 98.5pct ternary recipe.

Phase 3 #14: sandwich norm on trit-emb baseline. val 4.3662 vs g011 4.3731 = -0.007 nats (noise). Step 500 worse (6.57 vs 6.11): the extra norms disrupt very early training but converge to ~same. No measurable benefit at fast-A scale. Wall +4pct from extra norm compute. Discard.

Phase 5 ASYMPTOTE: trit-emb + unfrozen scales + share-kv + bf16 m + lr=0.15 + eff=64 + 10000 steps. val 4.0090. CLOSES THE GAP: 98.5pct ternary model at 10k steps matches g007 (FP-emb, eff=64, 5k steps) val 4.0085 within 0.0005 nats. Trajectory still descending at end (-0.019 last 500). Wall 33380s (~9h). The Phase 5 recipe is essentially as good as FP-emb at extended compute. NEW Phase 5 champion.
